---
title: "Southeast Asia Energy Consumption Visualization Project"
author: "Group 2"
date: "2026-07-13"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default]
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
# Global setup chunk for libraries, options, and shared objects.
```

## Project Overview

This presentation documents a critique and improvement of an existing visualization of Southeast Asia's energy consumption patterns.

## Project Objectives

1. Apply data analysis techniques to generate meaningful insights from structured data.
2. Document data and code to ensure clarity, transparency, and reproducibility.
3. Collaborate effectively as a team to execute a complete, version-controlled data workflow.

## Data Context

- Source dataset: IEA, *Southeast Asia Energy Outlook 2026* — "Total final consumption mix by fuel and end-use sector in Southeast Asia, 2024"
- Scope: 10 ASEAN countries, 2024 cross-sectional snapshot only (no time series)
- Structure: long-format `energy_data.csv` with `Country`, `Dimension` ("Fuel"/"Sector"), `Category`, and `Percentage` fields
- Extraction: no downloadable data table existed; values were reconstructed from the chart's interactive hover tooltips and validated so each country-dimension pair sums to 100%
- **Important note: due to data quality issues at the source (e.g. inconsistent country naming conventions such as "Lao DPR" and "Viet Nam"), the original data could not be used as-is. We therefore built a structurally faithful synthetic proxy dataset that mirrors the real structure and distributions for this project.**
- Known limitations: Timor-Leste is absent (unavailable at source); Thailand's "Other Fuels" category is inconsistently reported across countries

### Existing Chart

![Original IEA Chart — Total Final Consumption mix by fuel and end-use sector in Southeast Asia, 2024](images/total-final-consumption-mix-by-fuel-and-end-use-sector-in-southeast-asia-2024.png)

- Dual-panel, 100% stacked column chart comparing **fuel supply mix** (left) against **end-use sector demand** (right) across all 10 ASEAN nations
- Strengths: enables direct part-to-whole comparison of energy "DNA" across diverse economies on a shared baseline; tells a clear story of oil/transport dependency vs. bioenergy-to-electricity development divide
- Weaknesses:
  - No on-chart legend — colors cannot be decoded without external report tables
  - Two different category systems (fuel vs. sector) shown side by side increases cognitive load
  - Percentages hide absolute consumption volume differences between countries
  - Non-baseline stacked segments (e.g. coal, electricity) are hard to compare across countries
  - Heavy reliance on color with minimal annotation slows interpretation and reduces accessibility

## Visualization Critique

### Key Issues to Address

- [Issue 1]
- [Issue 2]
- [Issue 3]

## Analysis Plan

- [Describe the descriptive analysis to be performed.]
- [Describe any comparisons, summaries, or validation checks.]
- [Describe the insight questions the team wants to answer.]

## Design Direction

- [Describe the improved chart concept.]
- [Describe the intended encodings, layout, and annotations.]
- [Describe accessibility and readability considerations.]

## Reproducible Workflow

- Data cleaning and validation
- Analysis and summary tables
- Visualization generation
- Quarto rendering to HTML

## Deliverables

- `presentation.qmd`
- Supporting data and scripts
- Exported HTML presentation

## Notes

- Fill in detailed findings, figures, and final conclusions later.
- Keep all paths relative to the project root.
